task: ok I think we are getting close to the point of diminishing returns with these reviews on this PR. What are your thoughts, one more round or lets merge this sucker and deploy?
<bash-input>git switch main && git pull</bash-input>

ok, take a look through @.planning/DEFERRED.md, as well as the phase docs from the last ~10 phases (28 -> 37) and identify if any deferred items exist.

also, update the @.planning/FEATURES.md tables with the latest features as well as update the feature matrix (batch uploads are not implemented in the rust SDK for example). 

The E2E test coverage in the feature matrix probably also needs an update.

You can also update the @README.md since it seems like a lot of that is now out of date.

One more piece of documentation to be added is the actual CipherBox file system specification which has not previously been documented, but should be done so now, for future reference. This doc should include both the various file system rules as well as the rationale for why these rules were implemented. Overall I am guessing we will need to stick with the strictest Desktop OS format (windows with its case insensitive naming).

All if this is to be done on a docs branch.
user_feedback_text:
ok, take a look through @.planning/DEFERRED.md, as well as the phase docs from the last ~10 phases (28 -> 37) and identify if any deferred items exist.

also, update the @.planning/FEATURES.md tables with the latest features as well as update the feature matrix (batch uploads are not implemented in the rust SDK for example). 

The E2E test coverage in the feature matrix probably also needs an update.

You can also update the @README.md since it seems like a lot of that is now out of date.

One more piece of documentation to be added is the actual CipherBox file system specification which has not previously been documented, but should be done so now, for future reference. This doc should include both the various file system rules as well as the rationale for why these rules were implemented. Overall I am guessing we will need to stick with the strictest Desktop OS format (windows with its case insensitive naming).

All if this is to be done on a docs branch.

task: A lot of those `deferred` items have been implemented already - for example the error tracking service, possible sentry migration path, session replay should not be in deferred, it is an anti-feature in a privacy focused product. Please go through the list again and make sure that the items in the list are actually deferred and not either already implemented, or not ever in scope.
user_feedback_text: A lot of those `deferred` items have been implemented already - for example the error tracking service, possible sentry migration path, session replay should not be in deferred, it is an anti-feature in a privacy focused product. Please go through the list again and make sure that the items in the list are actually deferred and not either already implemented, or not ever in scope.

task:
The mention of lack of Desktop FUSE automated tests is also misleading - there aren't unit tests as these would require mocking too much of the host OS to be reliable. Those are intentionally not tested at a unit level, since it is mostly jsut plumbing between the OS and the rust SDK. These components are recularly tested in the `Desktop E2E` test suite, so saying there is no automated testing is false, its just not unit testing.

The source for this in @.planning/codebase/CONCERNS.md should also be updated as irrelevant (wont fix) with the details of the above note.
user_feedback_text:
The mention of lack of Desktop FUSE automated tests is also misleading - there aren't unit tests as these would require mocking too much of the host OS to be reliable. Those are intentionally not tested at a unit level, since it is mostly jsut plumbing between the OS and the rust SDK. These components are recularly tested in the `Desktop E2E` test suite, so saying there is no automated testing is false, its just not unit testing.

The source for this in @.planning/codebase/CONCERNS.md should also be updated as irrelevant (wont fix) with the details of the above note.

task: the filesystem doc mentions AES-CTR for large files, but then goes on to mention only video, AES CTR is also used for audio files.
user_feedback_text: the filesystem doc mentions AES-CTR for large files, but then goes on to mention only video, AES CTR is also used for audio files.

task: also, in the same doc, there is mention of uploads always using AES GCM - this is false, large files are encrypted using AES-CTR client side.
user_feedback_text: also, in the same doc, there is mention of uploads always using AES GCM - this is false, large files are encrypted using AES-CTR client side.
